nodes.context: Street gang in Philadelphia, active in early 1950s, specialized in armed robbery, kidnapping, weapons dealings.
Another group of teenagers in Philadelphia using the same name, operating in West Philadelphia, not affiliated with the original Green Street Counts.

edges.evidence_quote: The Green Street Counts (West) were not affiliated with the Green Street Counts originally from the Fairmount section of Philadelphia.
edges.context: The text explicitly states they were not affiliated, meaning no relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: After their release the Counts would later connect with the K&A Gang of Philadelphia's Kensington section.
edges.context: The Green Street Counts connected with the K&A Gang after release from prison.
